project.name: UltraLite
project.description: Trip-centric gear management app. Users create bags (backpack + items), associate with trips, duplicate for reuse.

columns.nickname: TEXT (optional, defaults to email prefix on registration)

users.notes: Nickname is optional, defaults to email prefix (before @) on registration

columns.user_id: UUID FOREIGN KEY → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
columns.name: TEXT
columns.backpack_gear_item_id: UUID FOREIGN KEY → gear_items(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT

constraints: backpack_gear_item_id MUST reference gear_items WHERE category='backpack'
bags.notes: total_weight_grams is auto-calculated, never set manually

bag_items.description: Join table: what's packed in each bag

columns.bag_id: UUID FOREIGN KEY → bags(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
columns.gear_item_id: UUID FOREIGN KEY → gear_items(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

bag_items.notes: Same item can't be added twice to bag, use quantity field instead

columns.user_id: UUID FOREIGN KEY → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
columns.name: TEXT

columns.trip_id: UUID FOREIGN KEY → trips(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
columns.bag_id: UUID FOREIGN KEY → bags(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

columns.trip_id: UUID FOREIGN KEY → trips(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

user_gear_ownership.description: Tracks which gear items users own

columns.user_id: UUID FOREIGN KEY → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
columns.gear_item_id: UUID FOREIGN KEY → gear_items(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

user_gear_ownership.notes: Used in Gear Catalog feature for 'Mark as Owned' functionality

waitlist.description: Public email signups for coming soon page
waitlist.notes: RLS enabled, anonymous users can INSERT

bags_with_weight.description: Bags with calculated total_weight_grams + backpack details
bags_with_weight.usage: CRITICAL: Use this view instead of raw bags table for reads
bags_with_weight.sql:
SELECT b.id, b.user_id, b.name, b.backpack_gear_item_id,
  gi_backpack.brand AS backpack_brand,
  gi_backpack.model AS backpack_model,
  gi_backpack.weight_grams AS backpack_weight_grams,
  gi_backpack.image_url AS backpack_image_url,
  b.description, b.created_at, b.updated_at,
  COALESCE(gi_backpack.weight_grams, 0) + COALESCE(
    (SELECT SUM(COALESCE(bi.override_weight_grams, gi.weight_grams, 0) * bi.quantity)
     FROM bag_items bi JOIN gear_items gi ON bi.gear_item_id = gi.id
     WHERE bi.bag_id = b.id), 0
  ) AS total_weight_grams
FROM bags b JOIN gear_items gi_backpack ON b.backpack_gear_item_id = gi_backpack.id
bags_with_weight.notes: Includes backpack_image_url for displaying backpack product images in UI

bag_contents.description: Detailed bag items with calculated weights
bag_contents.usage: CRITICAL: Use this view for fetching bag items with details
bag_contents.sql:
SELECT bi.bag_id, b.name AS bag_name, gi.id AS gear_item_id,
  gi.brand, gi.model, gi.category, gi.weight_grams AS catalog_weight_grams,
  gi.image_url, bi.quantity, bi.override_weight_grams,
  COALESCE(bi.override_weight_grams, gi.weight_grams, 0) * bi.quantity AS total_item_weight_grams
FROM bag_items bi
JOIN bags b ON bi.bag_id = b.id
JOIN gear_items gi ON bi.gear_item_id = gi.id
bag_contents.notes: Includes gi.image_url for displaying item thumbnails in BagBuilder

bags_total_weight_auto_update.critical: Never manually set total_weight_grams - trigger handles it

constraints.backpack_selection: Only gear_items with category='backpack' can be used in bags.backpack_gear_item_id
constraints.no_duplicate_items: Same item can't be added twice to bag (UNIQUE constraint on bag_id, gear_item_id)
constraints.cascade_deletes: Deleting user cascades to bags/trips; deleting bag cascades to bag_items/trip_bags
constraints.gear_protection: Gear items protected (ON DELETE RESTRICT) if referenced in bags.backpack_gear_item_id

structure.server.js: Entry point: mounts routes, CORS, security middleware, runs on port 3000

auth.type: JWT tokens in Authorization: Bearer <token> header

nickname_requirements.allowed: Letters, numbers, spaces, underscores, hyphens only

auth.supports: Both direct PostgreSQL and Supabase Auth (configurable)

security.cors: Restricted to allowed origins only (ultralite.app, localhost:5173, vercel.app)

POST /api/auth/register.notes: Rate-limited, nickname optional (defaults to email prefix)

GET /api/gear.notes: Always applies LIMIT (defaults to 24 if not provided). Only applies OFFSET if > 0

GET /api/gear/owned.response: Array of user's owned gear item IDs

POST /api/gear/:id/toggle-owned.notes: Toggles ownership status for the gear item

GET /api/bags.response: Array of user's bags (uses bags_with_weight view)

GET /api/bags/:id.response: Bag object + items array (uses bag_contents view)

POST /api/bags/:id/duplicate.response: New bag with all items copied
POST /api/bags/:id/duplicate.notes: Uses TRANSACTION to copy bag + all bag_items

POST /api/bags/:id/items.notes: ON CONFLICT increments quantity if item exists

POST /api/trips/:id/bags.notes: Associate bag with trip, role defaults to 'primary'

environment.USE_SUPABASE: false (set true to enable Supabase Auth)

structure.App.jsx: Router, auth state, protected routes, Supabase listener

hooks/.useScrollReveal.js: Scroll reveal animations via Intersection Observer
hooks/.useImageBackgroundColor.js: Extracts dominant background color from images

components/.Navbar.jsx: Top nav (bags/trips/gear links, logout, shows nickname)
components/.GearCard.jsx: Reusable gear item card with image, weight, category badge
components/.GearDetailModal.jsx: Full gear details modal with 'Mark as Owned' toggle
components/.Toast.jsx: Fixed bottom-center toast; auto-dismiss + manual dismiss
components/.CommunityTripsStacking.jsx: Stacking cards for Community Trips on landing

pages/.ComingSoon.jsx: Coming soon page with email waitlist signup
pages/.Login.jsx: Enhanced login/register with nickname, password confirmation, show/hide toggle
pages/.Landing.jsx: Hero, features, Community Trips stacking section, pricing, testimonials
pages/.BagList.jsx: Grid of user bags with backpack images
pages/.BagBuilder.jsx: Create bag OR edit bag (2-column layout)
pages/.TripList.jsx: List trips, inline create form with DatePicker
pages/.GearCatalog.jsx: Browse all gear items (card/table view, pagination)

public./: Landing.jsx OR ComingSoon.jsx (if VITE_COMING_SOON=true) OR redirect to /bags or /login

useImageBackgroundColor.purpose: Extracts dominant background color from gear item images
how_it_works: Samples corner pixels (where background colors typically appear)
Groups similar colors and finds dominant color
Returns RGB string for use as CSS background color
Falls back to var(--color-neutral-100) if extraction fails
useImageBackgroundColor.usage: Used in GearCard, GearDetailModal, BagBuilder (GearThumbnail component)
useImageBackgroundColor.benefits: Creates seamless visual integration where gear images blend into card backgrounds
useImageBackgroundColor.cors: Uses crossOrigin='anonymous' for public Supabase bucket images

ConfirmModal.behavior: ESC and click overlay to close

CloneModal.description: Fixed overlay prompt dialog with single input

Toast.usage: Delete/clone failure messages on BagList and TripList

DatePicker.behavior: Trigger styled as .input; click opens calendar popover (month grid, prev/next month). Outside click and ESC close.
DatePicker.usage: TripList create form (Start Date, End Date). End Date receives min={start_date} for validation.

features: Centered search bar with focus on open
Category filter pills (horizontal scroll); respects restrictCategory
No results shown until user types or selects category
Paginated results: 10 items per page with 'Load More' button
GearSearchModal.performance: Only loads initial 10 items, fetches more on demand

CommunityTripsStacking.description: Stacking sticky cards for Community Trips on landing page
CommunityTripsStacking.behavior: View locks when section top aligns with navbar (first card top: 5rem, then 5rem + index * 20px)
CommunityTripsStacking.display: Left: trip details + user nickname + optional weight. Right: backpack image or placeholder.
CommunityTripsStacking.notes: Currently uses mockCommunityTrips in Landing.jsx. Replace with API call when backend ready.

ComingSoon.description: Coming soon page with email waitlist signup

features: Grid of bags with backpack thumbnails (56x56)
Fallback placeholder icon if no backpack image

BagBuilder.left_column: Bag summary + item list with 'Add Gear' button
BagBuilder.thumbnails: Use useImageBackgroundColor hook for seamless backgrounds
features: Real-time weight updates after adding/removing items
List trips with inline create form
DatePicker for Start Date and End Date (end min=start)
Card grid view (default) or table view toggle
Proper pagination (24 items per page) with page numbers
Resets to page 1 when search/category changes

auth_flow: 1. Login/Register → receives token + user (with nickname) → stored in localStorage['token']
2. App.jsx checks token on mount → calls /api/auth/me → sets user state
3. api/client.js interceptor adds token to all requests
4. Protected routes check user state, redirect to /login if null
5. Logout → clear localStorage → clear Supabase session (if enabled) → clear user state
6. If Supabase enabled, auth state changes are listened to in App.jsx

auth: register(email, password, nickname?) // nickname optional, defaults to email prefix
gear: getGear({search?, category?, limit?, offset?}) // returns {items[], total, limit, offset}
getUserOwnedGear() // returns user's owned gear item IDs (protected)
toggleGearOwnership(gearItemId) // toggle owned status, returns {owned: bool}
bags: getBags() // uses bags_with_weight view on backend
getBag(id) // includes items via bag_contents view

state_management.pattern: Each page fetches own data on mount
state_management.mutations: Refetch after mutations (e.g., add item → reload bag)

styling.framework: Tailwind CSS via CDN in index.html
styling.approach: Mobile-first responsive: sm:, md:, lg: breakpoints

production_vercel.VITE_COMING_SOON: true (set in Vercel dashboard to show coming soon page)

steps: 1. Copy bag row with new name
2. Copy all bag_items rows with new bag_id

weight_calculation.override: Respects override_weight_grams if user provides custom weight

gear_item_usage.backpacks: Only category='backpack' can be used in bags.backpack_gear_item_id
gear_item_usage.other_gear: All other gear goes in bag_items
gear_item_usage.no_duplicates: Same item can't be added twice (UNIQUE constraint) → use quantity field

user_data_isolation.all_queries: Filter by user_id from JWT token
user_data_isolation.access_control: Users can only access own bags/trips

add_gear_to_bag.frontend:
await addItemToBag(bagId, gearItemId, 1);
loadBag(); // refetch to get updated weight
add_gear_to_bag.backend: Route handles: verification bag belongs to user, ON CONFLICT increments quantity if item exists, trigger updates total_weight_grams

query_patterns.trip_with_bags:
SELECT t.*, b.name, bw.total_weight_grams, tb.role
FROM trips t
JOIN trip_bags tb ON t.id = tb.trip_id
JOIN bags b ON tb.bag_id = b.id
JOIN bags_with_weight bw ON b.id = bw.id
WHERE t.id = $1;

design_system.overview: Modern B2B SaaS aesthetic inspired by AllTrails/Ramp. Nature-inspired color palette.

animations.scroll_reveal: Uses Intersection Observer for fade-in on scroll

design_system.customization: Edit CSS variables in src/index.css to change color scheme

backend.infrastructure: Self-hosted Dell XPS laptop (Ubuntu Server 24.04) exposed via api.ultralite.app
backend.flow: Internet → api.ultralite.app:443 → Router (port forward) → Nginx (SSL termination) → Docker container :3000

server_details.host: Dell XPS laptop running headless Ubuntu Server 24.04
server_details.domain: api.ultralite.app (A record points to server's public IP)
server_details.ssl: Let's Encrypt cert via Certbot, auto-renewed

docker.dockerfile:
FROM node:20-slim

# Build deps for bcrypt native compilation
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    python3 make g++ && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app
RUN groupadd --gid 1001 nodejs && \
    useradd --uid 1001 --gid nodejs --shell /bin/bash --create-home nodejs

ENV NODE_ENV=production
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --omit=dev
COPY . .
RUN chown -R nodejs:nodejs /app
USER nodejs
EXPOSE 3000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD node -e "fetch('http://localhost:3000/api/health').then(r => process.exit(r.ok ? 0 : 1)).catch(() => process.exit(1))"

CMD ["node", "server.js"]
docker.compose_file:
version: "3.8"
services:
  api:
    image: ghcr.io/arjuna26/ultralite/ultralite-backend:latest
    container_name: ultralite-backend
    restart: always
    network_mode: host  # Required for IPv6 DNS resolution to Supabase
    env_file:
      - .env
docker.network_mode_note: network_mode: host is required because Supabase database hostname resolves to IPv6 only, and Docker's default bridge network doesn't support IPv6

ci_cd.triggers: Push to main when backend/** changes
ci_cd.process: Builds Docker image and pushes to GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io)
ci_cd.workflow_yaml:
name: Build and Push to GHCR
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths: ['backend/**']

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: ${{ github.repository }}/ultralite-backend

jobs:
  build-and-push:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: ./backend
          push: true
          tags: |
            ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:latest
            ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:${{ github.sha }}

auto_update.behavior: Only restarts container if image actually changed
auto_update.note: Watchtower was attempted but incompatible with Docker 29.x (API version mismatch)

backend.dynamic_dns: Script auto-updates Porkbun A record when public IP changes (cron or systemd timer)

local_dev_workaround.issue: NAT hairpinning - requests to api.ultralite.app fail from same LAN
local_dev_workaround.note: Only affects local network; external users and Vercel work normally

management_commands.force_pull: docker compose pull && docker compose up -d

environment.variables:
DB_HOST=db.xxxxx.supabase.co
DB_USER=postgres
DB_PASSWORD=...
DB_NAME=postgres
DB_PORT=5432
JWT_SECRET=...
USE_SUPABASE=true
SUPABASE_URL=https://xxxxx.supabase.co
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=...

supabase_integration.overview: Auth system supports both direct PostgreSQL and Supabase Auth. Toggle with environment variables.

setup.step_2_backend: Add to backend/.env: USE_SUPABASE=true, SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY. Install: cd backend && npm install @supabase/supabase-js
setup.step_3_frontend: Add to frontend/.env: VITE_USE_SUPABASE=true, VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY. Install: cd frontend && npm install @supabase/supabase-js
setup.step_4_migration: If using Supabase, user data stored in auth.users with metadata. For custom PostgreSQL, run: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS nickname TEXT;

how_it_works: If true and credentials exist, Supabase client is initialized
Auth routes check isSupabaseEnabled() to choose backend
Frontend still uses same API endpoints (backend handles the routing)
JWT tokens are generated by backend for API access regardless of auth provider

security.anon_key: Safe for frontend, protected by Row Level Security
security.jwt_secret: Still required for API token generation

recent_changes.details: Added hook to extract dominant background color from gear item images by sampling corner pixels. Returns RGB color string for seamless card backgrounds. Handles CORS for public Supabase bucket images. Updated GearCard, GearDetailModal, and BagBuilder components.

Frontend: Replaced infinite scroll 'Load More' with proper pagination in GearCatalog. Shows 24 items per page with page number controls, Previous/Next buttons. Resets to page 1 when search or category filter changes. Backend: Fixed /api/gear endpoint to always apply LIMIT (defaults to 24). Only applies OFFSET when > 0.

Added CommunityTripsStacking.jsx – stacking sticky cards for 'Community Trips' on landing page. View locks when section top aligns with navbar. Left = trip details + user nickname + optional weight. Right = backpack image or placeholder. Landing.jsx placeholder section renders component. When backend ready, replace mock with getCommunityTrips() API call.

Added custom popups without new dependencies. ConfirmModal for confirmations (delete), CloneModal for single-input prompts (clone name), Toast for auto-dismiss notifications. Same overlay + card + button pattern. BagList and TripList updated to use these instead of alert/confirm/prompt.

New reusable DatePicker component in components/DatePicker.jsx. Input-style trigger, calendar popover with month nav, value as YYYY-MM-DD, optional min/max. Used in TripList create form (Start Date, End Date with min validation).
Refactored to accept optional restrictCategory (e.g., 'backpack') and optional title for reuse in different contexts (e.g., backpack-only picker with custom title).

requirements: Join trips → trip_bags (primary or first bag per trip) → bags → bags_with_weight (for total_weight_grams, backpack_image_url, backpack_brand, backpack_model) → users (for nickname only)
Do NOT expose email or other private user data
Ensure query selects bw.backpack_image_url from bags_with_weight (existing trip detail route doesn't include this)

agent_instructions: Use views (bags_with_weight, bag_contents) for reads, not raw tables
Never manually set total_weight_grams - trigger handles it
All user routes must filter by req.user.userId from JWT
Use quantity field for bag items, don't add duplicates
Use transactions (BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK) for multi-step DB operations
After mutations, refetch data to see updated weights
Return data from views, not raw tables in API responses
Use try-catch for errors, log them, return appropriate HTTP codes
Use parameterized queries only, validate user ownership for security
Always apply LIMIT in backend queries to prevent loading all items (default 24 items per page if limit not provided)
Use useImageBackgroundColor hook for gear images to create seamless card backgrounds
Use ConfirmModal for confirmations (e.g., delete) and CloneModal for single-input prompts (e.g., clone name). Use Toast or inline error for API failure messages instead of alert()
Use the reusable DatePicker component for date fields; value and min/max as YYYY-MM-DD
Modify AGENTS.md when making changes to keep record of the codebase for future work

example_sql_insert.description: SQL to insert into gear_items table on Supabase
example_sql_insert.sql:
INSERT INTO gear_items (
  id,
  brand,
  model,
  category,
  subcategory,
  weight_grams,
  weight_source,
  capacity,
  materials,
  image_url,
  source,
  source_url
) VALUES (
  gen_random_uuid(),
  'Nemo', 
  'Tensor Regular-Wide',
  'backpack',
  'R5.4',
  620,
  'manufacturer',
  '1',
  '',
  'https://www.nemoequipment.com/cdn/shop/files/Tensor-AllSeason-RegularWide_811666035189_01-Main_1728x.jpg?v=1744132242',
  'manual',
  NULL
)
